situation: SZ-PP-M-003 from audit-2026-07-07-047: SettingsPage.tsx line 23 chrome.runtime.sendMessage({action:'getSettings'}).then(...) has no .catch handler. When background service worker is sleeping/unavailable (extension updating, idle timeout), promise rejects and setLoading(false) never fires → permanent loading spinner on settings page.
action: Added .catch(err => console.error(...)) and .finally(() => setLoading(false)) to guarantee loading state clears regardless of success/failure. Also added optional chaining res?.success to handle undefined responses. Single-file fix in popup worktree (route-fix). cp-guard triggered (prior SZ-PP-M-001/M-002 unmerged), used case-104 direct wt commit escape hatch.
outcome_evidence: commit 88d4c7b on auto/opt-popup-1783215325 pushed to origin; diff shows +4/-1 lines adding .catch/.finally chain to getSettings sendMessage call
next_suggestion: SZ-PP-M-004: Debounced saveSettings has no error handling and stale closure risk
SZ-PP-M-005: Auto-save debounce timer not flushed on unmount — last edit lost
SZ-PP-M-006: All chrome.runtime.sendMessage calls lack timeout

audit_findings.finding: chrome.runtime.sendMessage getSettings has no .catch — permanent loading state on rejection
audit_findings.remediation: Add .catch/.finally to guarantee setLoading(false) runs regardless of outcome
